text: The laparoscopic view reveals a large subserous fundal uterine fibroid. Vasopressin is injected into the uterus to reduce blood flow before proceeding with the myomectomy, performed without the use of a uterine manipulator.

The surgeon makes an oblique incision on the anterior surface of the large uterine fibroid. The fibroid is enucleated, and a smaller fibroid is also removed. The inner layer of the defect is sutured continuously using a V-lock barb suture.
The surgeon sutures the cirrhosa layer of the uterus using interrupted sutures, showing the appearance of the uterus after the completion of suturing.

The surgeon makes an incision on another subserous fibroid and enucleates it. The resulting defect is sutured in one layer with interrupted sutures. A bag is introduced into the abdominal cavity, and the fibroids are placed inside it for morcellation.
The surgeon applies anti-adhesion gel over the incisions on the uterus following the removal of fibroids. The excised fibroid specimen weighs 680 grams.
